name: ship
description_raw: Run the pre-launch checklist via parallel fan-out to specialist personas, then synthesize a go/no-go decision
description_normalized: Fan-out orchestrator that runs code-reviewer, security-auditor, and test-engineer in parallel then merges reports into a go/no-go shipping decision.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Run the pre-launch checklist via parallel fan-out to specialist personas, then synthesize a go/no-go decision

name: agent-orchestrator
description_raw: Design advisor for multi-agent orchestrated workflows using Claude Code teammate mode. Use when user wants to "design a multi-agent workflow", "set up agent orchestration", "spin up multiple agents", "build an agent pipeline", "orchestrate agents", "agent team workflow", "teammate mode setup", "how do I run multiple agents", "agent sequencing", "multi-agent gates".
description_normalized: Design advisor for multi-agent orchestrated workflows using Claude Code teammate mode.

name: ansible-fleet
description_raw: Manage and audit the Ansible-provisioned Mac fleet across David's machines (AppyDave group) and client machines (e.g. filtcore for Lars). Use when user says "audit fleet", "audit the machines", "check machine drift", "which machines have X", "add capability to ansible", "add this to the fleet", "remove from fleet", "deprecate capability", "ansible playbook", "fleet config", "group_vars", "host_vars", "fleet alignment", "machine out of sync", "what's on all my machines", "compare machines", "fleet inventory", "provision machine". The skill has three capabilities: (1) Fleet Audit — produce a drift/alignment report across all machines, (2) Capability Lifecycle — add/promote/deprecate a capability with Ansible-compatible classification, (3) Config Variation Management — explain where a setting lives (all/group/host).
description_normalized: Manage and audit the Ansible-provisioned Mac fleet across David's machines (AppyDave group) and client machines (e.g.

name: apps-registry
description_raw: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave app registry at ~/.config/appydave/apps.json. Use when the user says: "add app", "register app", "retire app", "archive app", "list apps", "find app", "what apps do I have", "apps by family", "apps by runtime", "matrix data", "registry stats", "apps for [machine]", "update app", "move app to family", "validate registry", or "migrate registry".
description_normalized: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave app registry at ~/.config/appydave/apps.json.

name: bmad-story-lifecycle
description_raw: Launches and manages the complete BMAD story lifecycle using Claude Code multi-agent teammate orchestration. Use when user wants to "run a BMAD story", "start the story lifecycle", "run story 6.6", "bmad story run", "orchestrate a story", "run the full BMAD pipeline", "start from Amelia", "skip Bob", "bmad orchestrate". Produces infrastructure start block, orchestrator system prompt, and paste-ready agent command sequence for a given story ID.
description_normalized: Launches and manages the complete BMAD story lifecycle using Claude Code multi-agent teammate orchestration.

name: clauding-lab-events
description_raw: Create recurring Clauding Lab events on app.sola.day for the 4Seas Community. Use when user says "create Clauding Lab event", "4C event", "4Cs event", "Claude Lab event", "Claude Lab Thursday", "Clauding Lab Thursday night", "sola.day event", or "schedule Claude Lab". Handles browser automation via Playwright MCP to fill and submit the event creation form.
description_normalized: Create recurring Clauding Lab events on app.sola.day for the 4Seas Community.

name: codebase-audit
description_raw: Three-lens codebase audit orchestrator — runs code quality, test quality, and architectural review in parallel as subagents and synthesises a combined readiness verdict. Use when user says "codebase audit", "audit the code", "run all three audits", "complete audit", "comprehensive audit", "code review audit", "is this codebase ready". For a layer-aware full system health check use appydave:system-audit instead. For single-lens audits use appydave:code-quality-audit, appydave:test-quality-audit, or appydave:architectural-review directly.
description_normalized: Three-lens codebase audit orchestrator — runs code quality, test quality, and architectural review in parallel as subagents and synthesises a combined readiness verdict.

name: conversation-triage
description_raw: Mid-conversation audit that surfaces open questions, loose threads, parked topics, decisions made, and unstated assumptions — right now, in the current session. Use when user says "what are the open items", "what did we decide", "what's unresolved", "loose ends", "what did we park", "what are we tracking", "what questions are still open", "triage the conversation", "what have we not finished", "where are we up to", "what did I ask that we never answered".
description_normalized: Mid-conversation audit that surfaces open questions, loose threads, parked topics, decisions made, and unstated assumptions — right now, in the current session.

name: dark-factory-catalog
description_raw: Catalog and assess skills, agents, workflows, commands, templates, and hooks across the 13 dark-factory cluster repos (BMAD-METHOD, gstack, Superpowers, Agent Skills Osmani, Compound Engineering, Compound Knowledge, Everything Claude Code, Ruflo, gbrain, Spec Kit, GSD, POEM, AppyDave Plugins). Distill ~1000+ raw artifacts down to ~50 unified skills under David's system. Use when user says "dark factory catalog", "catalog the repos", "recon BMAD" or "recon any specific repo", "discover artifacts", "discover skills across repos", "tag artifact stages", "evaluate compound engineering", "distill testing skills", "distill the catalog", "catalog self-improvement", "factory agent assessor", "assess skills across repos", "build the catalog", or any operation on the dark-factory cluster artifact corpus. Six capabilities (recon / discover / tag / evaluate / distill / self-improvement) — each invokable independently. Schema is NOT defined upfront — schema discovery is a first-class capability.
description_normalized: Catalog and assess skills, agents, workflows, commands, templates, and hooks across the 13 dark-factory cluster repos (BMAD-METHOD, gstack, Superpowers, Agent Skills Osmani, Compound Engineering, Compound Knowledge, Everything Claude Code, Ruflo, gbrain, Spec Kit, GSD, POEM, AppyDave Plugins).

name: delivery-review
description_raw: Orchestrate parallel delivery review across 6 quality dimensions. Launches review-blind-hunter, review-edge-case-hunter, review-acceptance-auditor, review-architecture, review-code-quality, and review-unit-tests as background agents, then triages and synthesizes findings into a unified verdict. Use when: 'delivery review', 'review this PR', 'review my changes', 'code review all dimensions', 'full review', 'review before merge', 'is this ready to ship'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate parallel delivery review across 6 quality dimensions.

name: doc-review
description_raw: Orchestrate parallel documentation review across 6 quality dimensions. Launches doc-review:coherence, doc-review:gaps, doc-review:completeness, doc-review:topology, doc-review:prose, and doc-review:crossref as background agents, then triages and synthesizes their findings. Use when user mentions: 'review docs', 'doc review', 'review this brain', 'documentation quality', 'full doc audit', 'review all dimensions', 'run doc review'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate parallel documentation review across 6 quality dimensions.

name: easy-git
description_raw: Safe, guided Git workflow for non-experts working across multiple machines. Use when the user wants to pull, push, sync, resolve conflicts, or check Git status without knowing Git commands. Triggers on phrases like "sync my repo", "push my changes", "I have merge conflicts", "pull latest", "git is broken", "rebase help", "can't push", "diverged branches", or any request to get a Git repo into a clean, synced state.
description_normalized: Safe, guided Git workflow for non-experts working across multiple machines.

name: five-personas
description_raw: Run a decision, problem, or idea through 5 independent cognitive lenses in parallel, then synthesise into a Chairman recommendation. Use when: 'five personas', 'run through the personas', 'stress test this idea', 'get five perspectives', 'challenge this decision', 'what am I missing', 'think about this differently
description_normalized: Run a decision, problem, or idea through 5 independent cognitive lenses in parallel, then synthesise into a Chairman recommendation.

name: grab-downloads
description_raw: Move and rename files from ~/Downloads into the current project. Use when user says "grab from downloads", "bring in that file", "import from downloads", "move that file here", "that PDF I downloaded", "the screenshot I just took", "file from my downloads", "pull from downloads", "get the file from downloads", or any phrasing suggesting they want to bring a downloaded file into the current working directory or project.
description_normalized: Move and rename files from ~/Downloads into the current project.

name: lexi
description_raw: Portable brain librarian — curate, onboard, maintain, and analyse second-brain knowledge collections (single brain or multi-brain collections). Use when setting up a new brain folder, onboarding a collection of brains, running a full health audit, adding frontmatter, populating INDEX.md, auditing a brain's compliance, syncing file counts, fixing tags, finding duplicate documents, identifying files that need splitting, finding isolated brains, finding orphan/unlinked files, auditing negative-knowledge coverage, adding aliases or tags to a brain, building the machine-readable brains-index.json, or enriching raw intake files. She introduces herself as Lexi. Trigger on phrases like "run lexi", "ask lexi", "set up a new brain", "onboard this brain", "onboard this collection", "initialize this folder", "run audit", "full audit", "audit the brain", "health check the brain", "fix the index", "clean up the brain", "check frontmatter", "add frontmatter", "sync file counts", "fix tags", "tag compliance", "what's missing in the brain", "sweep all brains", "audit the collection", "find duplicates", "search similar", "duplicate check", "find similar documents", "what's duplicated", "find documents to split", "check SRP", "single responsibility", "what needs splitting", "files that cover too much", "split candidates", "find isolated brains", "check isolation", "find orphan files", "find unlinked files", "check orphans", "check happy path", "find brains without anti-patterns", "negative knowledge audit", "add alias", "add tag", "build brains index", "rebuild brain index", "regenerate brains-index", "add brain alias", "add brain tag", "find brain by alias", "enrich raw files", "add frontmatter to intake", "health check raw intake", "normalise transcripts", "enrich transcripts".
description_normalized: Portable brain librarian — curate, onboard, maintain, and analyse second-brain knowledge collections (single brain or multi-brain collections).

name: mocha
description_raw: Mocha — the view renderer in the Mochaccino ecosystem. Takes JSON data files (from Peter) and renders self-contained HTML views using the AppyDave brand. Part of the Mochaccino quartet (with Mochaccino orchestrator, Peter, and Shelly). Use when Mochaccino delegates view work, or when user says "Mocha, render this", "render a view", "re-render", "create the HTML", "generate the designs", "update the view", "regenerate the gallery", "build the HTML from this data". Mocha is the artist — she never touches data files, only reads them.
description_normalized: Mocha — the view renderer in the Mochaccino ecosystem.

name: mochaccino
description_raw: Mochaccino — UI/UX design system orchestrator. Entry point for all design work. Manages the full design loop: interview → data → views → revisions → gallery. Works with three specialists: Peter (data), Mocha (view renderer), and Shelly (Shape Librarian). Use when anyone says "mochaccino", "mockup", "design a screen", "let's do a mochaccino", "I want to design something", "explore a UI idea", "wireframe this", "what would X look like", "mock up the UI for", "new workspace", "add a workspace", "show me the gallery", "list designs", "create a parallel workspace", "create a version 2", "start a new design session". Never bypassed — always the entry point even when delegating to Peter, Mocha, or Shelly.
description_normalized: Mochaccino — UI/UX design system orchestrator.

name: mochaccino-v1
description_raw: DEPRECATED — original single-persona Mochaccino skill. Preserved as a working reference while the new quartet system (mochaccino + mocha + peter + shelly) matures. Use mochaccino-v1 when you want the simpler, faster, self-contained HTML approach with no MVC separation. Use the new appydave:mochaccino for the full orchestrated pipeline. Trigger: "mochaccino-v1", "simple mockup", "quick mockup no data layer", "old mochaccino".
description_normalized: DEPRECATED — original single-persona Mochaccino skill.

name: near-compaction
description_raw: Orchestrate all context-saving actions when Claude's context window is filling up. Use when user says "near compaction", "running out of context", "context is filling up", "save everything before we compact", "pre-compaction save", "almost at limit", "context nearly full", or when you notice context warnings. Runs a triage checklist and calls leaf skills in the right order — session-checkpoint, capture-context, knowledge-capture — so nothing important is lost before compaction fires.
description_normalized: Orchestrate all context-saving actions when Claude's context window is filling up.

name: omi
description_raw: OMI pipeline orchestrator — syncs new transcripts from the OMI API then extracts and classifies them with Gemini. Use when user says: "sync and extract OMI", "run omi pipeline", "omi sync", "process omi", "omi fetch and extract", "catch up on omi", "run omi", "update omi", "update discovery queries", "refresh random context", "update omi queries".
description_normalized: OMI pipeline orchestrator — syncs new transcripts from the OMI API then extracts and classifies them with Gemini.

name: omi-schedule
description_raw: Manage the omi-sync launchd schedule on macOS. Use when user says "update omi schedule", "change omi sync times", "when does omi sync run", "set omi to run every X hours", "show omi schedule", "omi cron", "omi launchd", "update omi plist", or any request to view or change when the omi-sync agent fires. Generates/updates ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.appydave.omi-sync.plist and regenerates _omi-sync-schedule.json (in $OMI_INTAKE_DIR) as a human-readable observation file.
description_normalized: Manage the omi-sync launchd schedule on macOS.

name: peter
description_raw: Peter — data specialist for the Mochaccino ecosystem. Manages the Providence data lifecycle: create, refresh, snapshot, fork, and discover JSON data shapes. Part of the Mochaccino trio (with Mochaccino and Shelly). Use when Mochaccino asks Peter to create or update data, or when user says "ask Peter to", "update the data", "refresh the JSON", "create a data shape", "snapshot this", "fork the data", "Peter can you", "what does the data look like". All data files live in .mochaccino/data/ and follow strict schema conventions.
description_normalized: Peter — data specialist for the Mochaccino ecosystem.

name: prompt-injection-scanner
description_raw: Scan untrusted content for prompt injection attacks before it enters any pipeline. Wraps content in an isolated Opus agent for model-pinned, auditable sanitization. Use when: 'scan this for injection', 'sanitize this content', 'check this before processing', 'is this safe to process?', 'scan incoming email / OMI / document / search result', or any time external content (email, web, relay, uploaded files, client documents) is about to be processed by a downstream skill or pipeline.
description_normalized: Scan untrusted content for prompt injection attacks before it enters any pipeline.

name: prose2models
description_raw: Converts evolving prose into structured machine-readable models (UX pages, user journeys, data models, presentation structures). Use when you have prose you need structured for downstream tools, or when user says "parse this", "extract structure", "model this", "turn this into a UX model", "extract user journey", "update the model", "version the model", "what shapes can I extract from this", or "what's the next step on the model". Supports extract, update, version, shape-discovery, and guidance commands.
description_normalized: Converts evolving prose into structured machine-readable models (UX pages, user journeys, data models, presentation structures).

name: ralphy
description_raw: Ralphy — David's Ralph Wiggum approach for autonomous batch campaigns on existing projects. Use when user says "ralphy", "run ralphy", "start a ralph campaign", "new campaign", "new PR", "new worktree", "start a wave", "plan this", "build this", "extend this", "add features", "run a coordinator loop", "what does ralphy do", "switch to analysis", "switch to development", "switch to content", "switch profile", or any mention of running an autonomous batch of work (planning, building, extending) on an existing project. Ralphy has four modes: Requirements, Plan, Build, Extend — and three campaign profiles: Development, Analysis, Content. He is directive — he reads current state and tells the user where things are and what's available.
description_normalized: Ralphy — David's Ralph Wiggum approach for autonomous batch campaigns on existing projects.

name: remote-machines
description_raw: Run commands, copy files, or execute workflows on David's other machines via SSH. Use when user says "run this on the mac mini", "run it on the other machine", "ssh to mac-mini", "deploy to the agent machine", "bot machine", "headless machine", "workstation machine", "send this to mac-mini-m4", "mac-mini-m2", "remote machines", "other machines", or any request involving executing something on a machine other than the current one. Also use proactively when a task would clearly benefit from running on a specific machine (e.g. a background job on the headless agent machine).
description_normalized: Run commands, copy files, or execute workflows on David's other machines via SSH.

name: review-acceptance-auditor
description_raw: Verify that every acceptance criterion is actually met by the implementation — not just 'code exists' but 'intent is satisfied'. Maps ACs to code evidence. Use when: 'check ACs', 'acceptance audit', 'does this meet the spec', 'verify acceptance criteria', 'AC coverage'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (AA dimension).
description_normalized: Verify that every acceptance criterion is actually met by the implementation — not just 'code exists' but 'intent is satisfied'.

name: review-architecture
description_raw: Verify code changes align with the project's architecture document — patterns, conventions, boundaries, and technical decisions. Use when: 'architecture review', 'does this follow the architecture', 'check patterns', 'convention compliance', 'structural alignment'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (AR dimension).
description_normalized: Verify code changes align with the project's architecture document — patterns, conventions, boundaries, and technical decisions.

name: review-blind-hunter
description_raw: Adversarial review that finds subtle issues hiding in plain sight. Cynical, skeptical analysis of code changes — looks for what feels off even when it technically works. Use when: 'blind hunt', 'adversarial review', 'what am I missing', 'find hidden issues', 'cynical review'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (BH dimension).
description_normalized: Adversarial review that finds subtle issues hiding in plain sight.

name: review-code-quality
description_raw: Assess naming, structure, DRY, separation of concerns, and code craftsmanship in changed code. Fast, diff-scoped quality check — not a full audit. Use when: 'code quality review', 'is this clean', 'quality check', 'naming review', 'DRY check'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (CQ dimension).
description_normalized: Assess naming, structure, DRY, separation of concerns, and code craftsmanship in changed code.

name: review-edge-case-hunter
description_raw: Exhaustive edge case analysis — walks every branching path and boundary condition, reports only unhandled cases. Method-driven path enumeration, not intuition. Use when: 'edge cases', 'boundary conditions', 'what could break', 'missing validations', 'path analysis'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (EC dimension).
description_normalized: Exhaustive edge case analysis — walks every branching path and boundary condition, reports only unhandled cases.

name: ruflo
description_raw: AppyDave's curated wrapper around Ruflo (claude-flow v3.5+). Codifies install, prune, memory-init, status, swarm-generation, AND dispatches the pre-flight swarm-check gate. When invoked with argument `swarm check` / `swarm-check` (e.g. via `/appydave:ruflo swarm check`) — OR when user types "should this be a swarm", "what shape for this work", "before we start", "pre-flight", "what topology" — IMMEDIATELY read /Users/davidcruwys/dev/ad/appydave-plugins/appydave/commands/ruflo-swarm-check.md and execute its 4-question gate to produce a 3-line decision (Decision / Write-zones / Ruflo wrap). Do not redirect the user — execute the gate inline. Also use this skill when the user says "install ruflo", "set up ruflo", "ruflo init", "prune ruflo", "ruflo prune", "ruflo customize", "what does ruflo install", "ruflo status", "ruflo agents", "orient", "what agents do I have", "how do I use these agents", "where do I start", "what should I do next", "generate swarm", "swarm this", "create swarm brief", "spawn swarm for", "ruflo generate", "build swarm for", "design a swarm to", or describes a task and asks how to run it as a Ruflo swarm.
description_normalized: AppyDave's curated wrapper around Ruflo (claude-flow v3.5+).

name: schedules-registry
description_raw: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave schedules registry at ~/.config/appydave/schedules.json — the single source of truth for every scheduled job David runs (cron, launchd, Claude Code routines, ScheduleWakeup loops). Use when the user says: "add schedule", "register schedule", "list schedules", "what's running on cron", "what cron jobs do I have", "what's scheduled", "remove schedule", "schedule stats", "where does X run", "schedules on [machine]", "validate schedules", "audit schedules", or "show me my schedules".
description_normalized: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave schedules registry at ~/.config/appydave/schedules.json — the single source of truth for every scheduled job David runs (cron, launchd, Claude Code routines, ScheduleWakeup loops).

name: shelly
description_raw: Shelly — Shape Librarian for the Mochaccino ecosystem. Maintains two catalogs: shape patterns (known data structures and when to use them) and UI components (reusable elements extracted from designs). Part of the Mochaccino trio (with Mochaccino and Peter). Use when Mochaccino asks Shelly to suggest a shape, or when user says "ask Shelly", "what shape fits", "Shelly can you", "add this to the library", "extract this component", "what components do we have", "find a pattern for", "which shape should I use for". Shelly grows over time — new patterns are added from real usage.
description_normalized: Shelly — Shape Librarian for the Mochaccino ecosystem.

name: system-context
description_raw: Generate or refresh a CONTEXT.md system comprehension document for the current repository. Creates a deep synthesized comprehension document — not a summary — that eliminates repeated codebase crawls. External systems (brains, agents in other repos, scheduled jobs) read it once and understand the system deeply without touching source files. Use when user says "generate context", "system context", "create CONTEXT.md", "refresh context", "update CONTEXT.md", "system comprehension", "orient me", "what does this app do", "why does this app exist", "domain concepts", "scope limits", "document this project", "help me understand this codebase", "what is this repo", "is CONTEXT.md stale", "generate project docs", "create orientation doc", "system context committed", "shared system context", "system context for the team", "ship context with the repo".
description_normalized: Generate or refresh a CONTEXT.md system comprehension document for the current repository.

name: truth-trail
description_raw: Knowledge integrity rules system for second-brain agents. Enforces the four pillars — Canonical, Synthesis, Citation, Provenance Chain — whenever any agent writes a synthesis document (derivative created from a source of truth). Consult truth-trail before writing any _synth.md, session summary, brief, skill, doc, or other derivative document. description_normalized: Knowledge integrity rules system for second-brain agents.

Use when the user wants continuous autonomous operation, scheduled tasks, or a self-directing agent loop.

name: ask-user
description_raw: Reusable pattern for presenting the user with explicit choices and gating execution until they respond. Used by other skills when a decision point requires human input before proceeding. Platform-agnostic — works on Telegram (inline buttons), Discord, CLI, or any agent with a message tool.
description_normalized: Reusable pattern for presenting the user with explicit choices and gating execution until they respond.

name: cold-start
description_raw: Day-one data bootstrapping for a new brain. Sequences the highest-leverage data sources to go from empty brain to useful brain in one session. Uses ClawVisor for safe credential handling — the agent never holds raw API keys. Covers Gmail import, calendar sync, contacts seeding, X/Twitter archive, conversation imports, and file archives.
description_normalized: Day-one data bootstrapping for a new brain.

name: cron-scheduler
description_raw: Schedule management with staggering, quiet hours, and wake-up override. Validates schedules, prevents collisions, and gates delivery during quiet hours.
description_normalized: Schedule management with staggering, quiet hours, and wake-up override.

name: ingest
description_raw: Route content to specialized ingestion skills. Detects input type and delegates.
description_normalized: Route content to specialized ingestion skills.

name: minion-orchestrator
description_raw: Unified Minions skill for both deterministic shell jobs and LLM subagent orchestration. Replaces the older gbrain-jobs routing intent. Use when: submitting gbrain jobs, shell/background tasks, spawning subagents, checking progress, steering running work, pausing/resuming, parallel fan-out. One durable, observable, steerable queue interface.
description_normalized: Unified Minions skill for both deterministic shell jobs and LLM subagent orchestration.

name: setup
description_raw: Set up GBrain with auto-provision Supabase or PGLite, AGENTS.md injection, first import.
description_normalized: Set up GBrain with auto-provision Supabase or PGLite, AGENTS.

name: soul-audit
description_raw: 6-phase interactive interview that generates the agent's identity (SOUL.md), user profile (USER.md), access control (ACCESS_POLICY.md), and operational cadence (HEARTBEAT.md). Re-runnable anytime to update any section.
description_normalized: 6-phase interactive interview that generates the agent's identity (SOUL.

name: webhook-transforms
description_raw: Generic framework for converting external events (SMS, meetings, social mentions) into brain-ingestible signals. Define a transform function, register a webhook URL, and incoming events get processed through the brain pipeline.
description_normalized: Generic framework for converting external events (SMS, meetings, social mentions) into brain-ingestible signals.

name: calendar-to-brain
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Google Calendar events become searchable brain pages. Daily files with attendees, locations, and meeting prep context.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Google Calendar events become searchable brain pages. Daily files with attendees, locations, and meeting prep context.

name: credential-gateway
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Secure access to Gmail, Google Calendar, and other Google services. ClawVisor (recommended) or direct Google OAuth.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Secure access to Gmail, Google Calendar, and other Google services. ClawVisor (recommended) or direct Google OAuth.

name: email-to-brain
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Gmail messages flow into brain pages. Deterministic collector pulls emails, agent analyzes and enriches entities.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Gmail messages flow into brain pages. Deterministic collector pulls emails, agent analyzes and enriches entities.

name: meeting-sync
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Meeting transcripts from Circleback auto-import into brain pages with attendee detection and entity propagation.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Meeting transcripts from Circleback auto-import into brain pages with attendee detection and entity propagation.

name: ngrok-tunnel
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Fixed public URL for your brain (MCP server, voice agent, any service). One ngrok account, never changes.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Fixed public URL for your brain (MCP server, voice agent, any service). One ngrok account, never changes.

name: restart-sweep
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Detect Telegram messages dropped during OpenClaw gateway restarts. Reads OpenClaw session state, alerts on aborted-mid-run sessions and (opt-in) suspicious silence gaps. Cooldown-gated so repeat detections don't spam.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Detect Telegram messages dropped during OpenClaw gateway restarts. Reads OpenClaw session state, alerts on aborted-mid-run sessions and (opt-in) suspicious silence gaps. Cooldown-gated so repeat detections don't spam.

name: twilio-voice-brain
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Phone calls create brain pages via Twilio + voice pipeline + GBrain MCP. Two architectures -- OpenAI Realtime (turnkey) or DIY STT+LLM+TTS (full control). Callers talk, brain pages appear.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Phone calls create brain pages via Twilio + voice pipeline + GBrain MCP. Two architectures -- OpenAI Realtime (turnkey) or DIY STT+LLM+TTS (full control). Callers talk, brain pages appear.

name: x-to-brain
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Twitter timeline, mentions, and keyword monitoring flow into brain pages. Tracks deletions, engagement velocity, OCR on images, and real-time alerts.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Twitter timeline, mentions, and keyword monitoring flow into brain pages. Tracks deletions, engagement velocity, OCR on images, and real-time alerts.

name: gsd:autonomous
description_raw: Run all remaining phases autonomously — discuss→plan→execute per phase. Pauses only for user decisions (grey area acceptance, blockers, validation requests). Uses ROADMAP.md phase discovery and Skill() flat invocations for each phase command.
description_normalized: Runs all remaining milestone phases autonomously (discuss→plan→execute), pausing only for user decisions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:autonomous
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run all remaining phases autonomously — discuss→plan→execute per phase

name: gsd:do
description_raw: Route freeform text to the right GSD command automatically. Analyzes freeform natural language input and dispatches to the most appropriate GSD command. Never does the work itself.
description_normalized: Dispatches freeform natural language to the appropriate GSD slash command via intent routing.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:do
frontmatter_yaml.description: Route freeform text to the right GSD command automatically
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: <description of what you want to do>

name: gsd:execute-phase
description_raw: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization. Orchestrator stays lean: discover plans, analyze dependencies, group into waves, spawn subagents, collect results. Each subagent loads the full execute-plan context.
description_normalized: Executes all phase plans in dependency-ordered parallel waves using fresh-context gsd-executor subagents.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:execute-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization

name: gsd:list-workspaces
description_raw: List all GSD workspaces with status overview.
description_normalized: Lists all GSD workspaces with their current status overview.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:list-workspaces
frontmatter_yaml.description: List all GSD workspaces with status overview

name: gsd:manager
description_raw: Single-terminal command center for managing a milestone. Shows a dashboard of all phases with visual status indicators, recommends optimal next actions, and dispatches work — discuss runs inline, plan/execute run as background agents.
description_normalized: Single-terminal milestone command center with phase dashboard and intelligent work dispatching.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:manager
frontmatter_yaml.description: Single-terminal command center for managing a milestone

name: gsd:map-codebase
description_raw: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents. Uses parallel gsd-codebase-mapper agents for tech, arch, quality, and concerns focus areas.
description_normalized: Analyzes the codebase using parallel gsd-codebase-mapper agents across four focus dimensions, writing structured analysis documents.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:map-codebase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [optional: specific area to map, e.g., 'api' or 'auth']

name: gsd:next
description_raw: Automatically advance to the next logical step in the GSD workflow. Reads STATE.md, ROADMAP.md, and phase directories to determine what comes next. Designed for rapid multi-project workflows.
description_normalized: Detects current project state and auto-advances to the next logical GSD workflow step.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:next
frontmatter_yaml.description: Automatically advance to the next logical step in the GSD workflow

name: gsd:plan-phase
description_raw: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop. Orchestrates: 4 parallel phase-researcher agents → research synthesizer → planner → plan-checker (loop ≤3). Supports --auto, --research, --skip-research, --gaps, --skip-verify, --prd, --reviews, --text flags.
description_normalized: Orchestrates parallel research, planning, and verification to produce a PLAN.md for a phase.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:plan-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop

name: gsd:set-profile
description_raw: Switch model profile for GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget/inherit). Calls gsd-tools.cjs config-set-model-profile directly via bash.
description_normalized: Switches the model profile for all GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget/inherit) via gsd-tools config.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:set-profile
frontmatter_yaml.description: Switch model profile for GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget/inherit)

name: gsd:workstreams
description_raw: Manage parallel workstreams — list, create, switch, status, progress, complete, and resume. Supports subcommands for concurrent milestone work.
description_normalized: Manages parallel workstreams for concurrent milestone work with subcommands for list/create/switch/status/complete/resume.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:workstreams
frontmatter_yaml.description: Manage parallel workstreams — list, create, switch, status, progress, complete, and resume

name: gstack
description_raw:
Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate pages, interact with
elements, verify state, diff before/after, take annotated screenshots, test responsive
layouts, forms, uploads, dialogs, and capture bug evidence. Use when asked to open or
test a site, verify a deployment, dogfood a user flow, or file a bug with screenshots. (gstack)
description_normalized: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate pages, interact with

name: benchmark-models
description_raw:
Cross-model benchmark for gstack skills. Runs the same prompt through Claude,
GPT (via Codex CLI), and Gemini side-by-side — compares latency, tokens, cost,
and optionally quality via LLM judge. Answers "which model is actually best
for this skill?" with data instead of vibes. Separate from /benchmark, which
measures web page performance. Use when: "benchmark models", "compare models",
"which model is best for X", "cross-model comparison", "model shootout". (gstack)
description_normalized: Cross-model benchmark for gstack skills. Runs the same prompt through Claude,

name: claude
description_raw:
Claude Code CLI wrapper for non-Claude hosts - three modes. Review: independent
diff review via claude -p. Challenge: adversarial failure-mode review. Consult:
ask Claude about the repo with read-only file tools. Use when asked for "claude
review", "claude challenge", "ask claude", "second opinion from claude", or
"outside voice". (gstack)
description_normalized: Claude Code CLI wrapper for non-Claude hosts - three modes. Review: independent

name: codex
description_raw:
OpenAI Codex CLI wrapper — three modes. Code review: independent diff review via
codex review with pass/fail gate. Challenge: adversarial mode that tries to break
your code. Consult: ask codex anything with session continuity for follow-ups.
The "200 IQ autistic developer" second opinion. Use when asked to "codex review",
"codex challenge", "ask codex", "second opinion", or "consult codex". (gstack)
description_normalized: OpenAI Codex CLI wrapper — three modes. Code review: independent diff review via

name: open-gstack-browser
description_raw:
Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.
Opens a visible browser window where you can watch every action in real time.
The sidebar shows a live activity feed and chat. Anti-bot stealth built in.
Use when asked to "open gstack browser", "launch browser", "connect chrome",
"open chrome", "real browser", "launch chrome", "side panel", or "control my browser".
description_normalized: Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.

name: pair-agent
description_raw:
Pair a remote AI agent with your browser. One command generates a setup key and
prints instructions the other agent can follow to connect. Works with OpenClaw,
Hermes, Codex, Cursor, or any agent that can make HTTP requests. The remote agent
gets its own tab with scoped access (read+write by default, admin on request).
Use when asked to "pair agent", "connect agent", "share browser", "remote browser",
"let another agent use my browser", or "give browser access". (gstack)
description_normalized: Pair a remote AI agent with your browser. One command generates a setup key and

name: dispatching-parallel-agents
description_raw: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies. Delegates each independent problem domain to a focused subagent with precisely crafted context, runs them concurrently, then reviews and integrates the results.
description_normalized: Parallel subagent dispatch skill for independent tasks that can run concurrently without shared state.

frontmatter_yaml.name: dispatching-parallel-agents

name: subagent-driven-development
description_raw: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session. Dispatches fresh implementer subagent per task (receives full task text, never reads plan file), followed by two-stage review: spec compliance reviewer then code quality reviewer. Continuous execution — no check-ins between tasks. Ends with final code review and finishing-a-development-branch.
description_normalized: Continuous plan execution skill using fresh subagents per task with mandatory two-stage spec-then-quality review gates.

frontmatter_yaml.name: subagent-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
